headline: American Water Charitable Foundation Continues National Partnership During American Red Cross Month; Donates $250,000 for Disaster Relief
summary: The American Water Charitable Foundation, a philanthropic non-profit organization established by American Water (NYSE: AWK), the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the U.S., announced today that it has again donated $250,000 to the American Red Cross, continuing its membership in the Disaster Responder Program. Proactively funding disaster relief helps ensure the Red Cross is better prepared to meet the needs of those affected by disasters, both big and small, across the U

headline: New Jersey American Water and the American Water Charitable Foundation Provide Over $2.2 Million in Statewide Support to New Jersey Communities in 2025
summary: New Jersey American Water today announced that it, along with its employees, provided more than $1.6 million through grants, charitable contributions, and program support to New Jersey organizations last year. With an additional $509,500 from the American Water Charitable Foundation, total charitable support topped $2.2 million, as detailed in the company's 2025 Community Impact Report.

headline: AWK Indiana Unit Boosts Infrastructure With $246M Investment in 2025
summary: American Water Works' Indiana unit invests $246M in 2025 to upgrade water infrastructure, supporting reliability and rising customer demand.

raw.headline: AWK Indiana Unit Boosts Infrastructure With $246M Investment in 2025
raw.summary: American Water Works' Indiana unit invests $246M in 2025 to upgrade water infrastructure, supporting reliability and rising customer demand.

headline: Kentucky American Water to Upgrade Water Infrastructure in Lisa Drive Area of Lexington with $1.4 Million Investment
summary: Kentucky American Water is investing approximately $1.4 million to upgrade critical water infrastructure in Lexington on Lisa Court, from the start of Lisa Court to the end of Lisa Drive, and Allen Drive, from Lisa Court to Judy Lane. This project will also include Faye Circle, Carla Court, Mona Court and Lisa Circle.
